2605.07723.title: LLM hallucinations in the wild: Large-scale evidence from non-existent citations
2605.07723.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are known to generate plausible but false information across a wide range of contexts, yet the real-world magnitude and consequences of this hallucination problem remain poorly understood. Here we leverage a uniquely verifiable object - scientific citations - to audit 111 million references across 2.5 million papers in arXiv, bioRxiv, SSRN, and PubMed Central. We find a sharp rise in non-existent references following widespread LLM adoption, with a conservative estimate of 146,932 hallucinated citations in 2025 alone. These errors are diffusely embedded across many papers but especially pronounced in fields with rapid AI uptake, in manuscripts with linguistic signatures of AI-assisted writing, and among small and early-career author teams. At the same time, hallucinated references disproportionately assign credit to already prominent and male scholars, suggesting that LLM-generated errors may reinforce existing inequities in scientific recognition. Preprint moderation and journal publication processes capture only a fraction of these errors, suggesting that the spread of hallucinated content has outpaced existing safeguards. Together, these findings demonstrate that LLM hallucinations are infiltrating knowledge production at scale, threatening both the reliability and equity of future scientific discovery as human and AI systems draw on the existing literature.
2605.07723.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已知在各種情境中生成似是而非但實際上錯誤的信息，但這種幻覺問題在現實世界中的規模和後果仍然不甚了解。<lb/>在這裡，我們利用一個獨特的可驗證對象——科學引用——來審核arXiv、bioRxiv、SSRN和PubMed Central中2.5百萬篇論文的1.11億條參考文獻。<lb/>我們發現，在廣泛採用LLM之後，虛構引用的數量急劇上升，保守估計在2025年僅有146,932條幻覺引用。<lb/>這些錯誤廣泛分佈在許多論文中，但在快速採用AI的領域中尤其明顯，在具有AI輔助寫作語言特徵的手稿中，以及在小型和早期職業作者團隊中更為突出。<lb/>同時，幻覺引用不成比例地將功勞歸於已經知名的男性學者，這表明LLM生成的錯誤可能會加強科學認可中的現有不平等。<lb/>預印本審核和期刊出版過程僅捕捉到這些錯誤的一小部分，這表明幻覺內容的擴散已超過現有的安全措施。<lb/>總的來說，這些發現表明LLM幻覺正在大規模滲透知識生產，威脅到未來科學發現的可靠性和公平性，因為人類和AI系統依賴於現有文獻。
2605.07723.author: Zhenyue Zhao et.al.
2605.07723.authors: Zhenyue Zhao, Yihe Wang, Toby Stuart, Mathijs De Vaan, Paul Ginsparg, Yian Yin
